title: ansible
content:
This LXC container, named ansible, serves as a dedicated environment for running configuration management and automation tasks within the homelab. It is based on the Debian operating system and is allocated 512 MB of RAM and 1 core, providing a lightweight yet functional platform. The container utilizes approximately 15.58 GB of disk space, ensuring sufficient room for storing configuration files, Ansible playbooks, and necessary dependencies required for automation scripts.
The primary role of the ansible container is to act as the central automation engine for the homelab. By hosting Ansible, it allows for the standardized deployment, configuration, and management of other virtual machines, services, and infrastructure components. This setup centralizes the automation workflow, enabling streamlined operations, reduced manual errors, and consistent state management across the entire homelab environment.

content:
The gitea-mirror container, running on a Debian base within the homelab environment, is specifically configured to act as a synchronization service for Git repositories. Its primary function is to mirror data from an external source, likely GitHub or another remote Git host, ensuring a local, redundant copy of code and project history is maintained. This setup leverages the lightweight nature of an LXC container to efficiently manage the mirroring process, utilizing 2 cores and 2GB of RAM to handle data synchronization tasks effectively.
In the context of the homelab, this container serves a crucial data management role. By acting as a mirror, it provides a reliable backup and synchronization layer for critical code assets, allowing the user to maintain a local, independent copy of their repositories. This setup enhances data security and availability, providing a self-hosted solution for managing open-source project data and ensuring that essential development resources are always accessible within the local network.

title: Node: pve
content:
The server named pve serves as the core hypervisor node in this homelab environment, running the Proxmox Virtual Environment distribution. This machine is dedicated to managing and hosting multiple virtual machines and containers, providing a centralized platform for running various services and testing complex network configurations. It operates on a customized Linux kernel, which provides the stable and robust foundation necessary for virtualization tasks, allowing for the efficient partitioning and management of system resources.
The physical hardware supports the virtualization workload effectively. The node is powered by an Intel Core i5-8500T processor featuring six cores, which provides sufficient processing power for simultaneous VM operations. In terms of memory, the system has 31.0 GB of total RAM, with approximately 18.89 GB currently in use by the operating system and running services. The system utilizes a modern Linux kernel build, ensuring up-to-date stability and performance for all hosted virtual environments.

title: openwebui
content:
The openwebui container, running as an LXC instance with the name openwebui, serves as a dedicated environment for hosting a modern user interface. It operates on a Debian base, utilizing 16 GB of RAM and 3 CPU cores, providing ample resources for demanding applications. The container is provisioned with approximately 58.76 GB of disk space, ensuring sufficient storage for the necessary software and configuration files. This setup reflects a common practice in homelab environments where lightweight LXC containers are used to efficiently run specific services.
This container’s primary role is to provide an accessible graphical interface, fulfilling the “interface” tag, for interacting with AI or machine learning services, aligning with the ai and community-script tags. By running Open WebUI, the container acts as the front-end gateway, allowing the user to manage, chat with, and visualize the outputs of various local AI models running elsewhere in the homelab. It effectively bridges the technical AI backend with a user-friendly experience, centralizing the access point for complex AI workflows within the home setup.
